expect: Cross-domain cluster of 4 falsification results produces L3+ lesson about systematic analogy failure
actual: L-1099 written (L3, Sh=9): domain analogies systematically fail because swarm is discrete-regime, not continuous. 4 falsification results converge on same root cause.
diff: Expected L3+ from 2-domain falsification cluster: MET. Mechanism identified (discrete enforcement transitions invalidate continuous-math assumptions) is genuinely novel — not a restatement of any existing L3+/L4.
note: n=10 with 80% L3+ rate (8/10). Baseline threshold 15% — exceeded by 5.3x. F-EXP12 distillation method CONFIRMED: cross-domain clusters with (1) ≥2 domains, (2) no L4 parent, (3) identifiable cross-lesson mechanism produce L3+ at 80% rate. Boundary condition from S448 falsification holds: clusters WITHOUT these properties (single-domain, high-redundancy, existing L4 parent) fail.
